content.text: Building GenAI Backends with Node.js and Express

Express is the dominant web framework in the Node.js ecosystem. Its middleware model maps cleanly to GenAI API requirements — auth, rate limiting, request validation, and SSE streaming are each one middleware or route handler. TypeScript adds request/response schema safety without a separate validation library.

The key Node.js advantage for GenAI backends is that its event loop handles concurrent SSE streams efficiently without blocking — a single process can stream LLM responses to many users simultaneously while waiting on I/O.

attrs.alt: Node/Express GenAI backend request and middleware flow

content.text: , auth, rate-limit, and body validation before reaching the route handler. The handler builds the message array, calls
, and pipes each text delta to the response as an SSE event.

src/
├── app.ts             # Express app factory (no listen — testable)
├── server.ts          # entry point: app.listen
├── routes/
│   ├── chat.ts        # POST /chat/completions
│   └── health.ts      # GET /health, /ready
├── middleware/
│   ├── auth.ts        # JWT verification
│   ├── rateLimit.ts   # Redis-backed rate limiter
│   └── validate.ts    # Zod body validation
├── services/
│   └── llm.ts         # Anthropic client wrapper
└── config.ts          # env var loading with zod
Zod validates request bodies at runtime and infers TypeScript types so the route handler is typed without duplication:

immediately after setting SSE headers so the browser knows the stream has started.
Not handling stream errors in the for-await loop:
if the Anthropic SDK throws mid-stream (timeout, credit exhausted), the
loop propagates the error. Wrap it in
and write an SSE error event before
Using synchronous JWT verify in a hot path:
is CPU-bound. For high traffic, cache valid tokens in Redis with a short TTL to skip re-parsing on every request.
nginx buffers SSE by default. Without this header, users see nothing until the full response finishes.
use PM2 or a Docker container with a health check. Unhandled promise rejections in route handlers will crash an unguarded Node process.
Express provides a thin, composable layer for GenAI APIs. Keep middleware focused (auth, rate-limit, validate), use Zod to enforce request schemas at runtime, and stream LLM responses with
loop. The async event loop handles concurrent streams without blocking — no thread pool or worker processes needed for I/O-bound LLM calls.
